title: www_justice_gov_uk_courts_procedure_rules_civil_contents_frontmatter_Notes18_bb3f83c0bb.html
content: NOTES TO ACCOMPANY AUGUST 2000 18th MONTHLY SUPPLEMENT – Civil Procedure Rules – Justice UK Cookie Settings Are you OK with cookies? We use small files called ‘cookies’ on www.justice.gov.uk. Some are essential to make the site work, some help us to understand how we can improve your experience, and some are set by third parties. You can choose to turn off the non-essential cookies. Which cookies are you happy for us to use? I am OK with cookies Only use essential cookies Choose which cookies we use Close Choose which cookies we use Marketing cookies Off On Google Analytics Off On We use Google Analytics to measure how you use the website so we can improve it based on user needs. We do not allow Google Analytics to use or share the data about how you use this site. See our Google Analytics cookies Google Analytics Name Purpose Expires _ga These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 2 years _gid These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 24 hours _gat These help us to manage how we collect analytics when we have lots of visitors on the site at one time 10 minutes Google Analytics Events Off On We use Google Analytics events to measure clicks within the pages and extended usage of the site See our Google Analytics Events cookies Google Analytics Events Name Purpose Expires _ga These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 2 years Essential cookies Always on All users These cookies will always need to be on because they make our site work. See our All users cookies All users Name Purpose Expires wordpress_test_cookie This is used to test whether the browser accepts cookies When you close your browser PHPSESSID This is used to link your device to the information sent to the server from your browser. It is typically used to avoid you having to retype information when moving from one page to another. When you close your browser ccfw-banner-hidden Lets us know you have chosen which cookies are used so we can stop the cookie banner appearing when you return to the site. 1 year ccfw-gtm-allowed Lets us know you have accepted certain cookies so we can stop them loading when you return to the site. 1 year ccfw-time This is used to store the cookie expiry dates so we know when to ask you again for consent. 1 year ccfw_gtm_id This cookie is used to link the cookie consent banner to Google Tag Manager 1 year Logged in users These cookies apply when users log in. They will always be on because they make our site work. See our Logged in users cookies Logged in users Name Purpose Expires wordpress_[hash] This authenticates you when you log in to the admin area When you close your browser wordpress_logged_in This shows the site that you’re logged in and who you are so you can access the functions you need When you close your browser wordpress_sec If you are logged in as a site admin, this stores your authentication details. When you close your browser wp-settings-{time}-[UID] The number on the end [UID] is your individual user ID from the users database. 1 year > Save cookie preferences . contents » frontmatter » NOTES TO ACCOMPANY AUGUST 2000 18th MONTHLY SUPPLEMENT Menu ≡ NOTES TO ACCOMPANY AUGUST 2000 18th MONTHLY SUPPLEMENT New material: Part 54 – Judicial Review Rules Part 54 – Practice Direction – Judicial Review CCR Order 49, rule 6A – new paragraph (17A), (17B) and (17C) and new Practice Direction Pre-Action Protocol for Construction and Engineering Disputes Pre-Action Protocol for Defamation Amendments to existing material are included as follows: The following Rules are amended: 2.3(1) amended 3.7 amended 6.5 amended 7.11 inserted 13.4 amended 19.4A inserted 24.3(2) amended 26.11 inserted 27.12 and 27.13 revoked 27.14(2)(c) amended 30.3(2) amended 30.8 revoked 33.9 inserted 36.6(5) amended 36.21(6) amended 39.3(4) amended 47.17(3 amended 52.1(2) amended RSC Order 53 revoked RSC Order 57 revoked RSC Order 64 rule 4(b) revoked RSC Order 98 rule 2(3) amended CCR Order 49 rule 6 revoked CCR Order 49 rule 6A(17A-C) inserted Practice Directions: Part 2B paras 3.1(c) amended, paras 7A and 15 inserted Part 7 para 2.10 inserted Part 8B para 2.1 amended, insertion after Table 2 Part 16 paras 2.1, 2.2, 11.4 and 11.5 inserted Part 19 paras 6.1 to 6.6 inserted plus attachment Part 19B paras 3.3 and 3.4 amended Part 25B paras 2A.1 and 2A.2 inserted Part 26 para 10.2(11) amended Part 27 paras 8.1 to 8.10 deleted, new paras 8.1, 8.2 and 8.3 inserted Part 28 para 1.2 amended Part 29 para 1.2 amended Part 30 para 7 inserted Part 39 para 8.1 inserted Part 40 para 14.4 inserted Part 42 para 2.6(3) amended Part 44 para 13.10 amended Part 52 paras 5.1A, 5.1B, 5.8A, 5.8B, 7.3A and 8.10 inserted, para 5.5, 5.6 and 8.9 amended RSC Order 52 and CCR Order 29 Committal Applications PD replaced RSC Order 53 delete PD RSC Order 54 para 6.1 amended Welsh Devolution title to PD amended, Part III introductory para amended, para 2.2 deleted, para 10.3(4)(c) amended, paras 14.1 to 14.4 deleted, para 14.1 inserted Insolvency Proceedings PD para 17.22(2)(d) amended, para 17.25 inserted, Directors Disqualification PD Annex 1 Cardiff amended Pre-Action Protocol para 4A.1 heading inserted, para 5.1 to 5.4 replaced Costs Practice Direction correction of cross references Transitional provisions Where a person has, before 2 nd October 2000, filed a notice of appeal in a claim allocated to the small claims track- Part 52 shall not apply to the appeal to which that notice relates; and rules 27.12 and 27.13 shall apply to that appeal as if they had not been revoked. Where a person has, before 2nd October 2000, filed an application for permission to make an application for judicial review in accordance with RSC Order 53- Part 54 shall not apply to that application for permission or the application for judicial review to which it relates; and RSC Order 53 shall apply to those applications as if it had not been revoked. Amendments or new material comes in to force on 2 October 2000 Updated: Monday, 30 January 2017 Top ↑ Home Courts Courts Procedure rules Civil contents frontmatter NOTES TO ACCOMPANY AUGUST 2000 18th MONTHLY SUPPLEMENT Accessibility Cookies Contacts Copyright Help Privacy Webchats Website queries Citizen and business advice GOV UK For citizen and business advice on justice, rights and more visit GOV.UK © Crown copyright
